message:
security: sendgrid + twilio connector URL / config hardening

Extends the recent connector origin-pinning pattern (jira / linear /
stripe / google-* / gmail / notion / asana / discord / whatsapp /
google-drive / google-admin-reports) to the SendGrid and Twilio
connectors.

- connectors/sendgrid/src/connector.rs: base_url override rejected if
  it carries a query, fragment, or userinfo component, matching the
  same-shape checks on sibling connectors. Provisioning recipe updated
  to pin the default api.sendgrid.com origin and reject schemes other
  than https. Adds negative-path tests for userinfo / query-string /
  fragment injection and for non-HTTPS configuration attempts.

- connectors/twilio/src/connector.rs: Twilio config now validates the
  base_url (and, where applicable, the per-region sub-account URL)
  against the same origin-pinning rule, plus additional rejection of
  empty / whitespace-only auth tokens in configure(). Adds regression
  tests that exercise userinfo injection, trailing-slash normalization,
  and the empty-auth-token refusal.

Both changes are defense-in-depth: the connector URL surface is
hardened to reject configuration shapes that could otherwise be used
to tunnel traffic through attacker-controlled origins or bypass the
origin-pin invariant assumed elsewhere in the rate limiter, audit
trail, and metric scope keys.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
